supported.deployment: single Go process behind production TLS termination
browsers: Chrome on the protected Ubuntu runner

known_limitations: Sessions and operator state are volatile process memory; restart logs users out and there is no shared-session failover.
TLS termination, secret injection, supervision, provider backups, and provider HTTP handler bindings are deployment-owner responsibilities.
Studio is fixed to provider contract 1.1.0 at the pinned provider commit and does not negotiate contract versions.
Only Chrome on protected Ubuntu CI is release-certified; Firefox and Safari are not certified for v1.0.0.
Metadata relationship behavior is limited to fields in the frozen contract and is never inferred from names.
Usage and discrepancy pages are observational and are not synchronous quota-enforcement authority.
Audit details are not rendered or exported beyond bounded redacted fields and redacted paths.
Studio exposes no password, token, certificate, raw SQL, workflow payload/result, worker identity, or fencing-token administration.
The release is source/commit based and makes no reproducible binary or container-image claim.
